D10 Imaging L5 Evaluate Reference

Lift · Gamma · Gain

three colour wheels each target a tonal zone — lift in shadows, gamma in midtones, gain in highlights; their hue offsets are non-orthogonal and overlapping

The grading primaries: lift adds a chroma offset weighted toward shadows (1 - x), gamma weighted toward midtones (4x(1−x)), gain weighted toward highlights (x). Push each wheel by a hue and amount and the curves modify each tonal zone differently — but the weight functions overlap, so any wheel touches every zone a little.

Drag the three hue sliders and amount sliders. The tone curve plot at right shows how each channel (R, G, B) gets reshaped. The graded patch grid below shows the result on a Macbeth chart.
